channel.title: computer-science on Mahmood's blog
channel.description: Recent content in computer-science on Mahmood's blog

item.title: data structures
item.description: \(\DeclareMathOperator{\spn}{span}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\dom}{domain}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\ran}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\rng}{range}\) \(\DeclareMathOperator{\img}{Im}\) \(\newcommand\dif[1]{\:\textrm{d}#1}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ceil{\lceil}{\rceil}\) \(\DeclarePairedDelimiter\floor{\lfloor}{\rfloor}\) 1 properties of floor for every 2 normal numbers \(m,n\) and every real number \(x\) \[ \ceil*{\frac{\ceil*{\frac{x}{m}}}{n}} = \ceil*{\frac{x}{m \cdot n}} \qquad \floor*{\frac{\floor*{\frac{x}{m}}}{n}} = \floor*{\frac{x}{m \cdot n}} \] \[ \ceil*{\frac{\ceil*{\frac{x}{2^i}}}{2}} = \ceil*{\frac{x}{2^{i+1}}} \qquad \floor*{\frac{\floor*{\frac{x}{2^i}}}{2}} = \floor*{\frac{x}{2^{i+1}}} \] the following is true for every normal number \(n\) and any number \(x\) \begin{gather} \floor*{x}+n = \floor*{x+n} \qquad \ceil*{x}+n=\ceil*{x+n}\\ n-\floor*{x} = \ceil*{n-x} \qquad n-\ceil*{x} = \floor*{n-x}\\ \floor*{x} \geq \ceil*{x}-1 \qquad \ceil*{x} \leq \floor*{x}+1\\ \floor*{x} > x-1 \qquad \ceil*{x} < x+1\\ \floor*{x} \leq x \qquad \ceil*{x} \geq x\\ \floor*{x} = n \iff n \leq x < n+1 \qquad \ceil*{x} = n \iff n-1 < x \leq n \end{gather}
